notes: Reverse-engineered the source PNG into a 10-room single-floor .floorplan (50ft x 32ft footprint): top public band (Foyer 8x12, Living 16x12, Dining 12x12, Kitchen 14x12), central Hall 50x4 corridor, bottom private band (Bedroom2 12x16, Bath2/Hall Bath 8x16, Bedroom3 12x16, Master 12x16, MasterBath/Ensuite 6x16). All room dimensions and labels were directly readable from the source so absolute positioning was used verbatim. Walls set to window on the top of Living/Dining/Kitchen and on the exterior bedroom faces matching visible glazing in the source. Doors connect Foyer/Living/Kitchen down to Hall and Hall up to all four private rooms plus Master->MasterBath; door percentages staggered to avoid wall overlap. Validation passed (10 rooms, 0 errors). compare_visual.py similarity 0.9944 (meanL1 0.00564, diffPct 1.33%) — well above the 0.70 digital-source threshold; remaining diff is mostly anti-aliasing on door arcs and minor door-position offsets along the Hall (exact percentages were estimated visually).
